summary: A dangerous, attack-minded side under Vincenzo Montella with an exciting cast of creators — they take the game to opponents. Back at a World Cup after 24 years, two years on from nearly upsetting the Netherlands in the Euro 2024 quarter-final.
style: Build from a back three with full-backs/wing-backs pushing on and a single deep midfielder (Ismail Yüksek) for the short pass. Best progressors Arda Güler and Hakan Çalhanoğlu drop deep to pull opponents out of shape and spear incisive passes; the approach play centres on isolating Kenan Yıldız 1v1 on the wing. No true No 9 since Burak Yılmaz retired — wingers rotate to occupy centre-backs while midfielders time late runs into the box.

counterReliance.note: Proactive, possession-and-rotation side that takes the game to opponents

strengths: Elite creative quality — Güler and Çalhanoğlu drop deep to orchestrate with pinpoint passing
Kenan Yıldız isolated 1v1 (most take-ons in Serie A last season)
Set pieces — Çalhanoğlu is one of the world's best dead-ball takers; ~37% of qualifying goals from set plays
Late runs into the box from Çalhanoğlu and Kökçü onto crosses/cutbacks
weaknesses: No recognised centre-forward — relies on wingers and midfield runners for goals

keyPlayers.name: Arda Güler
keyPlayers.note: Directs Turkey's possession — drops into pockets to receive and create from anywhere; 8.7 xA and 9 assists in La Liga, elite on-ball involvement and creative threat

keyPlayers.name: Kenan Yıldız
keyPlayers.note: Slippery, fearless dribbler — the focal point of the wide isolation play

keyPlayers.name: Hakan Çalhanoğlu
keyPlayers.note: Deep-lying orchestrator and world-class set-piece taker

tactics: Build from a three, drop the distributors deep to break lines, and isolate Yıldız to run at his man; punish from Çalhanoğlu's set pieces. Their attacking fluidity can inflict real damage in a balanced group.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group D (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
